title: The Stranger
author: Albert Camus
content: “I opened myself to the gentle indifference of the world.”
action: Detach from external emotional validation; view reality as neutral.

title: Waiting for Godot
author: Samuel Beckett
content: “They give birth astride of a grave, the light gleams an instant, then it's night once more.”
action: Avoid paralysis by ignoring external triggers that never materialize.

title: The Trial
author: Franz Kafka
content: “Logic may indeed be unshakable, but it cannot withstand a man who is determined to live.”
action: Prioritize individual agency over bureaucratic logic.

title: No Exit
author: Jean-Paul Sartre
action: Secure personal boundaries against unpredictable external variables.

title: The Myth of Sisyphus
author: Albert Camus
action: Find satisfaction in the rigors of process rather than outcome.
